page: Amazon Web Services c4.large is a compute-optimized instance belonging to the fourth-generation c4 family. It is powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2666 v3 processor running at 2.9 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The instance provides 2 dedicated vCPUs derived from 1 physical core with hyper-threading enabled. It features 4.0 GB of system memory, yielding 1.88 GB per core, and operates on the Xen hypervisor. There is no local storage or GPU hardware bundled with this instance, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates average performance for single-core operations but poor multi-core and memory throughput. The single-core stress-ng score of 1478.68 and Geekbench single-core score of 1133.0 both fall into the middle 50% tier. Conversely, the multi-core stress-ng score of 1945.19 and Geekbench multi-core score of 1368.0 are in the bottom 10% tier, reflecting the limitations of the single physical core. Memory bandwidth is constrained, with read-only large block speeds at 13.03 GB/sec and write speeds at 11.47 GB/sec, both ranking in the bottom 10%.

Given the hardware profile, this instance is suitable for lightweight, single-threaded compute tasks, basic web serving, or small-scale utility workloads. It exhibits poor performance in database operations and Redis SET operations, making it less suitable for high-throughput database hosting. The lack of local storage requires external network-attached storage for data persistence. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the c4.large offers dedicated CPU resources on an older hardware generation, presenting a low-cost entry point for compute-bound tasks that do not require modern instruction sets like AVX-512 or high memory density.
description: Amazon Web Services c4.large is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring an Intel Xeon E5-2666 v3 processor running at 2.9 GHz. It provides 2 dedicated vCPUs from 1 physical core, 4.0 GB of memory, and a baseline network speed of 1 Gbps, operating under the Xen hypervisor with no local storage or GPU. Performance benchmarks show average single-core capabilities, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1133.0 and a single-threaded gzip compression speed of 24.27 GB/sec. However, multi-core and memory performance are limited, with multi-core Geekbench scores and memory bandwidth falling into the bottom 10% tier. This instance is best suited for lightweight, single-threaded compute workloads, basic utility tasks, and low-traffic web serving where dedicated CPU allocation is required on a legacy hardware class, though it is constrained for memory-intensive or database workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c4.large is a compute-optimized instance with 2 vCPUs, 4 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon E5-2666 v3 CPU, offering average single-core performance but limited multi-core capacity.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c4.large is a Xen-based compute-optimized instance with 2 vCPUs, 4 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon E5-2666 v3 processor.
tagline: A legacy compute-optimized instance featuring dedicated dual vCPUs and balanced single-core performance for lightweight utility workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon E5-2666 v3 processor with 2 dedicated vCPUs and 1 physical core
4.0 GB of system memory with a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps
Average single-core performance suitable for single-threaded tasks
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Low multi-core and memory bandwidth performance limits heavy parallel processing
Suited for lightweight web serving, utility scripts, and basic compute tasks
